era_context: Middle Bronze Age II continues. The Hyksos period in Egypt persists. Mesopotamia is in the final Old Babylonian phase. The Indus Valley Civilization is in decline or recently abandoned. Shang dynasty in China is consolidating control over the Yellow River valley.

events.title: No specific events confirmed for this year
events.description: No events of documented historical significance have been identified for 1938 BCE. The historical record for this year is effectively silent across all major civilizations and regions.

sources.name: Historiographic consensus on Middle Bronze Age chronology

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed (none listed).
historiographic_note: 1938 BCE is a year for which no contemporary documentation survives. This is typical for the Middle Bronze Age; historical precision at the annual level is not achievable for most of the 2nd millennium BCE outside of a few well-documented reigns or events.
